dependencies.relationship: CLI wraps Go SDK client for all API calls. Every CLI command creates an apikit.Client and calls the corresponding SDK method. The CLI reuses the SDK's typed response structs and *APIError for error handling.
Uses build-time TokenPrefix variable coordinated with the server build. The TokenPrefix determines the config directory path $HOME/.<prefix>/ and must match the server's compiled value.

task_groups.title: Implement config.go: CLIConfig, InitConfig, LoadConfig, SaveConfig, and ResolveField

subtasks.title: Implement CLIConfig struct, InitConfig, and LoadConfig
details: Create internal/cli/config.go; define CLIConfig struct with TOML tags: endpoint_url, user_id, api_key.

Implement InitConfig(configDir string) error: use os.MkdirAll(configDir, 0700); if config.toml missing, write hard-coded template string with os.WriteFile and mode 0600. Hard-coded template: 'endpoint_url = ""
user_id = ""
api_key = ""
'. Do not modify existing file.

Implement LoadConfig(configDir string) (*CLIConfig, error): read config.toml; parse via toml.Decode or toml.NewDecoder; on any parse error return (nil, fmt.Errorf("config file is unparseable: %w", parseErr)).

subtasks.title: Implement SaveConfig atomic write and ResolveField credential resolution
details: Implement SaveConfig(configDir string, cfg *CLIConfig) error: os.CreateTemp(configDir, "config*.toml"); immediately defer os.Remove(tmp.Name()); marshal via toml.NewEncoder(tmp).Encode(cfg); chmod tmp to 0600; close tmp; os.Rename(tmp.Name(), configPath). Return os.CreateTemp error immediately (no deferred remove registered yet).

Implement ResolveField(fieldName, flagName, flagValue string, flagChanged bool, envVarName, configValue string, required bool) (string, error): precedence: flagChanged+non-empty flagValue > os.Getenv(envVarName) non-empty > non-empty configValue > error (required=true) or ("",nil) (required=false). Canonical error message composed internally from fieldName, flagName, envVarName.

subtasks.title: Implement ResolveEndpointURL helper for auth-exempt commands
details: Implement ResolveEndpointURL(cmd *cobra.Command) string in internal/cli/config.go (or root.go): read --endpoint-url flag and its Changed state from cmd.Root().PersistentFlags(); load config file from default config path; call ResolveField with required=false; return empty string on any error or missing value.
Ensure ResolveEndpointURL never returns an error to the caller — always returns a string (empty or non-empty).
Document the intent: for auth-exempt commands that optionally need endpoint_url without going through PersistentPreRunE.

task_groups.title: Implement PersistentPreRunE hook on the root command

subtasks.title: Implement PersistentPreRunE with ordered validation steps 1–4
details: In internal/cli/root.go, set rootCmd.PersistentPreRunE to a function implementing the ordered steps.
Step 1: check cmd.Annotations["auth"] == "none" → return nil immediately (auth annotation reads from leaf cmd, not rootCmd).
Step 2: validate TokenPrefix != "" → return fmt.Errorf("TokenPrefix is empty: binary was built without a valid -ldflags TokenPrefix value") wrapped as exit-2 error.
Step 3: call os.UserHomeDir(); if error or empty → return error with message "cannot determine home directory: $HOME is not set or unresolvable".
Step 4: derive configDir as filepath.Join(homeDir, "."+TokenPrefix); call InitConfig(configDir).

subtasks.title: Implement PersistentPreRunE validation steps 5–7: config load, credential resolution, and client construction
details: Step 5: call LoadConfig(configDir); return error on failure.

Step 6: resolve endpoint_url (required=true) and api_key (required=true) via ResolveField using cmd.Flags().Changed() for flag detection; resolve user_id (required=false) storing empty string without error.

Step 7: construct apikit.Client via apikit.NewClient(endpointURL, apikit.WithAPIKey(apiKey)); store client under clientContextKey{} and resolvedUserID under userIDContextKey{} via context.WithValue; update cmd.SetContext(ctx).
Ensure cmd argument (leaf command) is used for flag checking and annotation reading, not rootCmd — guard against the common Cobra pitfall.

Call PrintJSON(output) on success; return nil (exit 0 even when server_version is omitted).

task_groups.title: Implement agent interface: help command and SetHelpFunc (help.go)

subtasks.title: Implement help --json command tree walker and JSON structure builder
details: Create internal/cli/help.go; implement walkCommands(cmd *cobra.Command) []CommandEntry recursively.
Collect only leaf commands: cmd.RunE != nil AND len(cmd.Annotations) > 0. Commands with both RunE and children are included as leaves.
For each leaf: extract full space-separated path (cmd.CommandPath()), description (cmd.Short), annotations (method, path, auth, composite), flags, args.

Flag descriptor: name ("--"+flag.Name), type (flag.Value.Type()), required (from cobra required annotation), description (flag.Usage). Omit 'default' if value equals type zero value ("" for string, 0 for int, false for bool, [] for stringSlice).
Annotation filter: commands with zero Annotations entries are excluded (Cobra built-ins).
Build root JSON object: {name: rootCmd.Use, version: Version, commands: [...]}}.

subtasks.title: Implement custom help subcommand and SetHelpFunc for per-command JSON help
details: In internal/cli/help.go: create custom helpCmd *cobra.Command that replaces Cobra's default help command (rootCmd.SetHelpCommand(helpCmd) or equivalent).

helpCmd.RunE: check if persistent --json flag is set via rootCmd.PersistentFlags().Changed("json"); if true, call walkCommands and PrintJSON the full tree (ignore positional args). If false, delegate to Cobra's default help behavior for any positional arguments.
Set rootCmd.SetHelpFunc to a function that checks the --json flag; if set, output single command JSON entry; else call defaultHelpFunc.
Ensure Cobra's completion command is NOT registered (shell completion is a non-goal); do not call rootCmd.InitDefaultCompletionCmd() or similar.
Consuming project commands added via AddCommand with at least one Annotations key automatically appear in walker output — no additional registration needed.

task_groups.title: Wiring verification: end-to-end integration, smoke tests, and stub audit

subtasks.title: Trace execution paths and verify function call chains are complete
details: Trace 13-PATH-1: verify main() → cli.Execute() → rootCmd.Execute() → PersistentPreRunE → InitConfig → LoadConfig → ResolveField → apikit.NewClient → context.WithValue → RunE → PrintJSON → os.Exit(0). No stubs or panic in any link.
Trace 13-PATH-2: verify API error path RunE returns *apikit.APIError → Execute() calls PrintError → code from apiErr.Code → os.Exit(1). Confirm no RunE calls PrintError directly.
Trace 13-PATH-3: verify missing credential path → ResolveField returns canonical error → PersistentPreRunE returns error → Execute() calls PrintError with code:0 → os.Exit(2).
Run smoke tests TS-13-SMOKE-1, TS-13-SMOKE-2, TS-13-SMOKE-3 with real components and mock SDK server.

subtasks.title: Trace version command and help --json execution paths; verify return value propagation
details: Trace 13-PATH-4: verify version RunE → PersistentPreRunE returns nil (auth:none) → ResolveEndpointURL → apikit.NewClient → client.Version(ctx) with bounded context → VersionOutput.ServerVersion populated → PrintJSON → os.Exit(0).
Trace 13-PATH-5: verify help RunE → --json detected → walkCommands traverses full tree → annotation filter applied → CommandEntry structs built → PrintJSON → os.Exit(0).

Verify return values propagate: walkCommands returns []CommandEntry to help RunE; VersionOutput.ServerVersion is *apikit.VersionResponse pointer (not copied struct); all callers use returned values rather than discarding them.

subtasks.title: Run atomic write smoke test and verify SaveConfig end-to-end wiring
details: Trace 13-PATH-6: verify spec-14/15 stub command → SaveConfig → os.CreateTemp in configDir → defer os.Remove → toml.NewEncoder.Encode → chmod 0600 → os.Rename → config.toml updated atomically → no temp files remain.
Verify deferred os.Remove on rename failure path: inject rename failure and confirm temp file is cleaned up and original config.toml is intact.
Run smoke test TS-13-SMOKE-6 with a stub spec-14/15 command calling SaveConfig directly.

subtasks.title: Dead-code and stub marker audit across all implementation files
details: Search all internal/cli/*.go and cmd/akc/main.go for Go stub markers: panic("not implemented"), // TODO (blocking), bare return nil in non-trivial functions, empty function bodies.
Confirm no Cobra completion command registration: grep -r 'InitDefaultCompletionCmd\|completion' internal/cli/ shows no registration calls.
Confirm SilenceErrors and SilenceUsage are set in code (not just tests): grep 'SilenceErrors\|SilenceUsage' internal/cli/root.go shows true assignments.
Confirm clientContextKey and userIDContextKey are unexported: grep -n 'type clientContextKey\|type userIDContextKey' internal/cli/context.go shows lowercase (unexported) type names.
